name: Stripe
one_liner: Online payment processing and financial infrastructure platform
auth_note: Secret API keys sent as Bearer credential; OAuth2 for Connect platforms and the remote MCP server.
access_note: Self-serve API keys with a full test/sandbox mode; live mode requires standard business account activation but no manual approval to develop.
mcp_note: Official remote MCP server at mcp.stripe.com (~23 billing/payments tools) plus docs search; verified on docs.stripe.com/mcp.
main_blocker: None; free self-serve test keys and broad REST API.

name: Plaid
one_liner: Bank-account data connectivity and financial account-linking API
auth_note: client_id + secret passed in body or PLAID-CLIENT-ID / PLAID-SECRET headers (JSON over HTTPS).
access_note: Free Sandbox with self-serve client_id/secret on signup; Production access requested via Dashboard and subject to compliance/approval review.
mcp_note: No confirmed official Plaid MCP server found.
main_blocker: Production access gated by Plaid approval and compliance review.

name: Binance
one_liner: Cryptocurrency exchange trading and market-data API
auth_note: API key plus signed payload; signature via HMAC-SHA256 (or Ed25519/RSA keys) on signed endpoints. REST + WebSocket + FIX.
access_note: Spot/Futures testnet keys are self-serve; production API keys require a KYC-verified Binance account, with regional/geo restrictions.
mcp_note: No official Binance MCP; several community Binance MCP servers exist.
main_blocker: KYC-verified account required for production keys; geo restrictions.

name: Paygent Connect
one_liner: Japanese online payment gateway with embedded checkout module
auth_note: Mutual-TLS client certificate plus merchant connection ID and connection password (configured in provided modules).
access_note: Requires a Paygent merchant contract (Japan); test and production module credentials/certs issued only after onboarding. No public self-serve API.
main_blocker: Merchant contract with Paygent required; Japan-only, no self-serve signup, docs behind onboarding.

name: iPayX
one_liner: FX forensic-audit MCP layer for cross-border payments
auth_note: Free tools (rate check, source compare, rate-limited audit) are unauthenticated; full forensic report requires a Bearer API key (paid per report).
access_note: Public MCP endpoint, no signup for free/rate-limited tools; paid forensic reports gated behind an API key. FINTRAC-registered.
mcp_note: The product IS an official MCP server (iPAYX-Technologies/mcp-fx-audit); marketed as first FX forensic-audit MCP. No conventional REST API; ipayx.ai/docs returns 404.
main_blocker: Narrow scope (FX audit only); full reports paid, but free tools are self-serve via public MCP.

name: QuickBooks
auth_note: OAuth 2.0 only; no API keys or basic auth.
access_note: Free developer account with up to 5 pre-populated sandbox companies; development self-serve, marketplace publishing needs Intuit review.
mcp_note: No Intuit-official MCP; community and Composio-hosted QuickBooks MCP available.

name: Xero
one_liner: Xero cloud accounting API for small businesses
auth_note: OAuth 2.0 required for all integrations; client id/secret from developer portal.
access_note: Free Xero account with a demo company and self-serve app registration; uncertified public apps capped at 25 connections until app-store certification.
main_blocker: None; demo company and app registration are self-serve.

name: Brex
auth_note: User API tokens (Bearer) self-serve for account/card admins; OAuth 2.0 for partner apps authenticating other Brex accounts.
access_note: Requires a Brex business account; admins self-serve create scoped API tokens under Settings > Developer. Partner OAuth credentials are application-gated.
mcp_note: Official Brex MCP documented at developer.brex.com/docs/mcp.
main_blocker: Requires an approved Brex business account (US business underwriting) to obtain tokens.

name: Ramp
one_liner: Corporate cards and finance-automation / spend-management API
auth_note: OAuth 2.0 with client_credentials (server-to-server) and authorization_code (delegated) grants; additive scopes.
access_note: Free self-serve sandbox at demo-api.ramp.com with its own OAuth app/credentials; production requires a Ramp business account and production app credentials.
mcp_note: Official Ramp MCP server (ramp-public/ramp_mcp) documented at docs.ramp.com.
main_blocker: Production needs an approved Ramp business account; sandbox itself is self-serve.

name: PitchBook
auth_note: API key / auth token issued by PitchBook's Direct Data team after contract.
access_note: Standalone paid enterprise contract via the Direct Data team; a sandbox is provided only to contracted clients. No self-serve signup.
mcp_note: No public MCP; Premium Connectors integrate ChatGPT/Perplexity for enterprise-licensed clients only.
main_blocker: Enterprise contact-sales gate; paid standalone license, no self-serve credentials.
